flashcards.definition: Layers of soil: O (organic), A (topsoil), B (subsoil), C (weathered parent material), R (bedrock).
Uppermost mineral layer; richest in organic matter and nutrients; essential for agriculture.
Removal of topsoil by wind and water; accelerated by deforestation, overgrazing, and poor farming practices.
Buildup of salts in soil from evaporation of irrigation water; reduces soil fertility.
1960s–70s agricultural transformation using high-yield varieties, fertilizers, pesticides, and irrigation.
